name: nat-pat
description: One NAT router (R1) with two private LANs and an outside router (R2) standing in for the internet. R1 runs static NAT for an inside server and PAT overload for an inside host, demonstrating all three inside-source NAT forms (static, pool, and overload) on Cisco IOS. Base for the NAT and PAT article.

nodes.name: R1
nodes.extra: static NAT 192.168.20.20->203.0.113.10; PAT overload of 192.168.10.0/24 onto pool PUBLIC (203.0.113.9); default route to R2

nodes.name: R2
nodes.extra: internet stand-in; route 203.0.113.8/29 back to R1

nodes.name: Host1
nodes.extra: PAT LAN; default route to R1

nodes.name: Host2
nodes.extra: inside server with static NAT; default route to R1
